title: Asphodelus infestus
taxonomy: Asphodelus infestus is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asphodelaceae. This family is a group of monocots that consists of plants with a single cotyledon in their seeds. Asphodelaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, including Asphodeloideae, which includes the genus Asphodelus. The genus Asphodelus itself is a group of plants commonly known as asphodels or asphodel flowers. The classification of Asphodelus infestus reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asphodelaceae family.

side_effects: Asphodelus infestus is considered toxic and can cause irritation, primarily affecting the skin and mucous membranes. Ingesting its parts can lead to severe gastrointestinal symptoms, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure may also result in eye irritation and phototoxicity. Furthermore, the plant contains asphodelin, a compound known to cause skin and eye irritation. Handling the plant with care is essential to minimize its potentially irritating effects.
